S|MPK |NS IS


SEEN '...tR RE-

Slightly Disguised, but Says He Does


Not Fear Arrest.

pages.raw_text:
Simpkins has no apparent fear
Of being arrested In spite Of the
big rewards talked OF. He says
the rinkertons do not want him
and that he Is Of no use to either
side.


And he denounces all Of Orch.
ards confession as q lie.


He admits being in Idaho the
time of the Steunenberg murder
and saw Orchard in Boise where
the latter claimed to be negotiating
the purchase of small ranch. In
Simpkins' opinion Orchard impli-
Gated him in his confessions to
give credence to them. Orchard
knew he was in Boise at the time
as an organizer of the federation,
and figured that people who other
wise would call Orchards confess
sion fake would believe it in part
on account of seeing Simpkins
around. The circumstance, he said,
would also serve to connect the
union with the murder.


About the time Orchards con-
fession was made and officers Of
the law were endeavoring so hard
to locate him, Simpkins was at
Phoenix, B. c., among friends. He
stopped for time at the Phoenix
hotel, conducted by Pat Shea, for
merly a policeman and miner in
Butte, Mont.


Representatives Of Thiele's De-
tecttve agency in this city got a
clew and when they reached
Phoenix Simpkins was out doing
assessment work with a couple of
friends. The Thiele men did not
express themselves as anxious to
do other than locate him. They
stated they did not seek his arrest.
